name: AlbumCoverGenerator
tagline: One-of-a-kind album covers from emojis, gradients, and text.
description: A small browser tool that turns emojis, gradients, and one line of bold text into a 3000x3000 album cover you can download as JPEG or PNG. Pick two colors, choose linear or radial, drop in any emoji palette, set rotation and opacity, add a title and artist, hit Download. No login, no signup, no AI generation. It won't replace a real designer; it'll replace 20 minutes of fiddling with stock templates when you just need a cover for a playlist or one-off mix.
first_comment:
Hey Hunters! I'm Moises, and I made AlbumCoverGenerator because every time I shipped a playlist or a one-off mix on Spotify, I got stuck for 20 minutes trying to design a cover that didn't look like a stock template. So I built the dumbest possible alternative: emojis, gradients, and one line of bold text on a 3000x3000 canvas.

The whole thing runs client-side. You pick two colors, choose a linear or radial gradient, drop in any emoji palette you like, and tune the count, rotation, and opacity. Then you add the album title and artist name, pick a font, and hit Download. There's a Shuffle Everything button if you want the dice to roll for you, and a debounced auto-regenerate so the canvas keeps up while you tweak sliders.

No accounts. No watermarks. No AI generation, so nothing weird shows up. Just deterministic, generative emoji art you can export as JPEG or PNG (with a 20MB guard before the download starts).

I'd love to hear: what other generative covers would you want? More layouts? Pattern overlays? Pixel art? Drop a comment with the kind of cover you wish existed and I'll build it next.
